name: Orange
summary: The classic vitamin C fruit — also a solid source of folate and the flavonoid hesperidin.

benefits.claim: Vitamin C for immunity and skin
benefits.mechanism: One orange covers most of a day's vitamin C, which immune cells depend on for function and which the body needs as a cofactor to synthesize collagen for skin, blood vessels, and wound healing.

citations.title: Vitamin C — NIH Office of Dietary Supplements (Health Professional Fact Sheet)

benefits.mechanism: Oranges provide folate, a B-vitamin essential for making DNA and new cells — especially important before and during pregnancy for healthy neural-tube development.

citations.title: Folate — NIH Office of Dietary Supplements (Health Professional Fact Sheet)

benefits.claim: Vitamin C is linked to better lung function
benefits.mechanism: The airway lining is constantly exposed to oxygen and inhaled oxidants, and vitamin C is a key antioxidant in the fluid that coats it. Higher dietary vitamin C tracks with better lung function, and a meta-analysis found vitamin C supplementation improved airflow (FEV1) in people with COPD.

citations.title: Efficacy of vitamin C supplementation on chronic obstructive pulmonary disease (COPD): a systematic review and meta-analysis (Lei et al., Int J Chron Obstruct Pulmon Dis 2022)

surprisingFacts.text: Most of an orange's flavonoids — like hesperidin — are concentrated in the white pith and membranes, so juice strained of pulp loses much of them.

cautions.effect: Very little. A lot of citrus at once can irritate the mouth or worsen reflux from the acid, and the fibre may loosen stools.
